name: Financial Conduct Authority
description: The Financial Conduct Authority regulates the conduct of financial services firms and financial markets in the United Kingdom.

name: Prudential Regulation Authority
description: The Prudential Regulation Authority is responsible for the prudential regulation and supervision of banks, building societies, credit unions, insurers and major investment firms in the United Kingdom.

name: European Commission
description: The European Commission proposes and enforces legislation across the European Union, including financial services regulation through directives and regulations.

name: Securities and Exchange Commission
description: The Securities and Exchange Commission protects investors, maintains fair and efficient markets, and facilitates capital formation in the United States.

name: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau
description: The Consumer Financial Protection Bureau is a regulatory agency responsible for consumer protection in the financial sector in the United States.

name: Information Commissioner's Office
description: The Information Commissioner's Office is the UK's independent supervisory authority for data protection and freedom of information rights.

name: UK Parliament
description: Primary legislation enacted by the UK Parliament. The Consumer Rights Act 2015 is enforced by the CMA and local authority Trading Standards services.

name: HM Treasury
description: HM Treasury is the government's economic and finance ministry, responsible for financial services policy and legislation.

name: Bank of England
description: The Bank of England is the central bank of the United Kingdom, responsible for monetary policy, financial stability, and supervision of payment systems.

name: European Banking Authority
description: The EBA is an independent EU authority working to ensure effective and consistent prudential regulation and supervision across the European banking sector.

name: European Securities and Markets Authority
description: ESMA is an independent EU authority that enhances investor protection and promotes stable and orderly financial markets.

name: European Central Bank (Banking Supervision)
description: The ECB's banking supervision arm (SSM) directly supervises significant banks in the euro area.

name: Financial Stability Board
description: The FSB monitors and makes recommendations about the global financial system, coordinating financial regulation across jurisdictions.

name: Bank for International Settlements / Basel Committee
description: The Basel Committee on Banking Supervision provides a forum for regular cooperation on banking supervisory matters. Its objective is to enhance understanding of key supervisory issues and improve the quality of banking supervision worldwide.

name: Financial Action Task Force
description: FATF is the global money laundering and terrorist financing watchdog, setting international standards to prevent these illegal activities.

name: International Swaps and Derivatives Association
description: ISDA fosters safe and efficient derivatives markets to facilitate effective risk management for all users of derivative products.

name: UK Finance
description: UK Finance represents nearly 300 of the leading firms providing finance, banking, markets and payments-related services in or from the UK.

name: International Capital Market Association
description: ICMA promotes the development of the international capital and securities markets, providing industry guidance, rules and recommendations.

name: FinExtra
description: FinExtra is a leading source of news and information about technology innovation in financial services.
